Name: Disillusioned
Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. While attached, that character cannot benefit from your affiliation auras. Consumed when the attached character transforms along a chosen-branch (Disillusioned) path.
Flavor: The banners she once saluted are only cloth now.
Notes: Canonical gate for the chosen-branch / wanderer transform. With Opportunity, enables Mage Arlia -> The Wandering Acolyte Arlia. 'Disillusioned' is a STATE; other events that confer it satisfy the same transform condition.

Name: Opportunity
Text: Play only if you control a Disillusioned character. Take one additional transformation action this turn.
Flavor: Every locked door has a moment it forgets to stay locked.
Notes: Catalyst half of the wanderer turn; pairs with Disillusioned. Generic value: an extra transformation action.

Name: A Crisis of Faith
Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. When this enters, draw a card. While attached, that character has +10 ATK and -10 DEF.
Flavor: Doubt does not make her careful. It makes her reckless.
Notes: Alternate Disillusioned source with an aggressive rider; satisfies chosen-branch transform conditions.

Name: Cast Out
Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. While attached, that character loses all Kingdom-internal affiliations.
Flavor: The order does not exile its own. It simply stops saying their names.
Notes: Harshest Disillusioned source; strips institutional chains and auras. Satisfies chosen-branch transform conditions.

Name: The Long Road
Text: Attach to a Disillusioned character or a Wandering character. At the start of each of your turns, that character heals 10. While attached, that character cannot bear equipment.
Flavor: The road provides. The road also takes.
Notes: Wanderer-line payoff and canonical heal-over-time example. Does NOT confer the Disillusioned state.

Name: Plague
Text: While Plague is in play, reduce the Max HP of all characters on both sides by 10. Cap-only rule applies.
Flavor: It does not ask which side you are on.
Notes: Persistent event (formerly a field card). Both-sides reach is carried in the text, not a category. Canonical Mathematical Opposition example and the canonical fall trigger ('Falls while Plague is in play').

Name: Medical Advancement
Text: While Medical Advancement is in play, increase the Max HP of your side's characters by 10.
Flavor: An honest kingdom answers death with bookkeeping.
Notes: Persistent event (formerly a field card). 'Your side' reach is carried in the text. Emergent counter to Plague's Max HP math; does not negate Plague's fall-trigger role.

Name: Call of the Channel
Text: Discard a card, then search your deck for a card with The Divine Channel affiliation, reveal it, add it to your hand, and shuffle your deck.
Notes: RECONSTRUCTED from v0.7 session (was not in the prior project CSV). Affiliation-locked tutor with a real cost (discard) per the tutoring design rule. Controller-side; no (G). CONFIRM exact wording, any element tag, and flavor. Now has real targets: The Ascended, Old Maid Hresheeba, Channel Being.

Name: Shield Wall
Text: Choose a Kaethlaan Knight you control. While Shield Wall is in play, that character gains DEF equal to its DEF minus 30 (minimum +0).
Notes: RECONSTRUCTED from v0.7 session. Renamed from 'Rally' to avoid collision with Banner of the Realm's Rally chain. Persistent Event framing is a deliberate workaround for the undefined one-shot buff-duration rule (open v0.7 question). Integer (DEF-30) buff avoids half-DEF breaking the x10 scale. CONFIRM wording (base vs current DEF), whether the Type should read 'Persistent Event / Kaethlaan', and flavor.

Name: Taken Prisoner
Text: Play only while a War is in play. Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes War-Torn. While attached, that character cannot attack. Consumed when the attached character transforms along a War-Torn path.
Flavor: The war did not kill him. It did something slower.
Notes: Costed state-gate modeled on Disillusioned, now gated behind an active War (you take prisoners during wars). Confers the War-Torn state (cannot attack); gates Swiftblade Kael -> Kael the Captured. Deliberate capture; gates Kael's outlaw road (Holy War's coin-flip capture does not). Also captures your own A Man Bred for War to buff him, and feeds The Broken March's War-Torn count.

Name: War
Text: While War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal 10 damage to each character in an active zone on either side. Leaders are not in the active zone and are unaffected. After War has been in play through two of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, War ends and is removed.
Flavor: It starts at a border and ends at a grave.
Notes: World-state War (carries the War keyword for 'while a War is in play' references). Active-zone-only attrition, both sides; Leaders exempt (separate slot). Self-removing burnout via coin flip from turn 2. No capture of its own. Anchor of the War family.

Name: Holy War
Text: When Holy War enters play, each player flips a coin; on heads, that player chooses one character they control, and it becomes War-Torn. While Holy War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal damage to each character in an active zone on either side: any character whose element includes Light (including Dark & Light hybrids) takes none; a character whose element is Dark and not also Light takes 20; all others take 10. Leaders are unaffected. After Holy War has been in play through four of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, Holy War ends and is removed.
Flavor: Every banner swears it fights for heaven.
Notes: World-state Light War. Now a CAPTURING war: on entry each player coin-flips and on heads makes one of their own characters War-Torn - the involuntary-ish War-Torn source that makes attack-through-War-Torn (Illyego, Unbroken Will) matter. This coin-flip capture does NOT gate Kael's outlaw road (that's still Taken Prisoner only). Attrition: Light-including spared, pure-Dark 20, others 10. Holds longer than War (burnout from turn 4).

Name: Goblin War
Text: While Goblin War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal 10 damage to each non-Goblin character in an active zone on either side. Goblin characters and Leaders are unaffected. After Goblin War has been in play through two of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, Goblin War ends and is removed.
Flavor: They do not take ground. They take everything, slowly.
Notes: Affiliation-scoped War (carries the War keyword). Damages non-Goblin active-zone characters on both sides; Goblins and Leaders exempt - a payoff for a Goblin deck rather than a smaller Holy War. Same burnout as War (from turn 2). Taken Prisoner works during it normally. Default: 10 damage; confirm.

Name: Crusade
Text: Play only while Holy War is in play. While Holy War is in play, your Light characters have +10 ATK and +10 DEF.
Flavor: The faithful do not fear the fire. They are the fire.
Notes: Wartime-buff family (Holy-War-specific, Light-locked). Stacks with Holy War sparing Light - intended crusader payoff; watch in playtest.

Name: Horde Frenzy
Text: Play only while a Goblin War is in play. While a Goblin War is in play, your Goblin characters have +10 ATK, and whenever one of your Goblin characters is KO'd, draw a card.
Flavor: More. There are always more.
Notes: Wartime-buff family (Goblin-War-specific, Goblin-locked). Stat + non-stat card draw on Goblin deaths (the expendable horde).

Name: Hardened Veterans
Text: Play only while a War is in play. While a War is in play, your Royal Army characters take no damage from War effects.
Flavor: They have buried every kind of friend. War is just weather now.
Notes: Wartime-buff family (any-war, Royal-Army-locked). Non-stat war-damage immunity - the strongest of the set; makes a war one-sided for a Royal Army deck. Flagged for balancing.

Name: Rally to War
Text: Play only while a War is in play. While a War is in play, your characters in an active zone have +10 ATK.
Flavor: The drum does not ask who wants to march.
Notes: Wartime-buff family (any-war, your-side). Active-zone aggression aura - rewards attacking into the war you started (those characters also eat the war's attrition).

Name: The Broken March
Text: Play only while a War is in play. While The Broken March is in play, your War-Torn characters may attack. Once per turn, if you control two or more War-Torn characters, you may have them attack one target together, dealing damage to that target equal to the sum of their ATK.
Flavor: The chains came off all at once.
Notes: War archetype finisher - the War-Torn chain the sim said the war deck lacked. Overrides War-Torn's cannot-attack and grants a War-Torn chain (sum of ATK to one target) when 2+ are out. Pairs with Taken Prisoner, Holy War captures, A Man Bred for War, Illyego. Turns War-Torn from pure cost into a build-around payoff.

Name: Metamorphosis
Text: Transform a T1 Wild you control into any T2 Wild in your hand. Does not use your transformation action. The new form keeps banked kills; its element becomes the new form's. Consumed on use.
Flavor: Everything wild is also becoming something.
Notes: One-shot Wild-deck enabler: the any-to-any morph (no fixed lineage) that reaches the six T2 Wild terminals. Does not count as the turn's transformation action, so you can morph and transform the same turn. Destination must be in hand. Future special cards may unlock other transformations the same way. Added this session.

Name: Close the Gates
Text: While in play, your Kaethlaan characters take no damage from World-state Wars.
Flavor: The walls remember every siege they outlasted.
Notes: Kaethlaan war-attrition immunity (parallel to Hardened Veterans).

Name: Reinforce the Front Lines
Text: Heal one of your Kaethlaan characters 20.
Flavor: Fresh legs, fed and angry.
Notes: One-shot Kaethlaan heal.

Name: War College
Text: Your Royal Army characters require one fewer item to transform.
Flavor: Doctrine is just other people's deaths, written down.
Notes: Transform-cost reducer for the Royal Army climb.

Name: Field Promotion
Text: Search your deck for a form one of your characters can transform into, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: The fastest way up is to be the one they cannot spare.
Notes: Tutor: fetches the next form for a body you control. War-deck consistency.

Name: War Effort
Text: Search your deck for a Destined or Faithless character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: Everyone serves. Some simply have not been asked yet.
Notes: Tutor: fetches a Kael (Destined) or Illyego (Faithless) character.

Name: Warren Muster
Text: Search your deck for a Goblin character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: The warren always has another to send.
Notes: Tutor: fetches a Goblin character. Goblin-deck consistency; parallels War Effort (affiliation-locked, slot cost). Added this session.

Name: Call of the Wild
Text: Search your deck for a Wild character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: The wild does not answer a summons. It answers a hunger.
Notes: Tutor: fetches a Wild character. Field Promotion cannot fetch Wild forms (they advance only via Metamorphosis), so this is how a Wild deck assembles the specific T2 terminal it will morph into. Added this session.

Name: Conscription Order
Text: Search your deck for a Royal Army character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: The realm does not ask. It enlists.
Notes: Tutor: fetches a Royal Army character. Loyalist climb consistency beyond the Divine Channel package (Call of the Channel). Affiliation-locked, slot cost. Added this session.

Name: Truce
Text: No attacks can be declared until your next turn. (Your combat this turn and your opponent's next combat are both skipped.)
Flavor: A line drawn in the mud that both sides pretend to honor.
Notes: Protection / stall tool (balance-log Round 9). Pushes the win condition toward T4/terminals by letting late-game decks survive a rush; an aggressor never wants it (forfeits its own attack). Engine: sets skipCombat on both players. AI plays it defensively when out-boarded.

Name: Sanctuary
Text: Until your next turn, a character you control cannot be attacked.
Flavor: Some ground is older than the war and will outlast it.
Notes: Protection family (balance-log Round 10). Single-target untargetable shield — surgical complement to Truce; protects one key climber/terminal/Leader. Engine: Unit.shielded, cleared at controller's next turn; excluded from attack + chain targeting.

Name: Bulwark
Text: Until your next turn, a character you control has +30 DEF.
Flavor: Set the shields. Let them break on us.
Notes: Protection family (balance-log Round 10). Single-target +30 DEF — the cheaper mitigation; saves a body when raising DEF past the incoming hit is enough. Engine: Unit.tempDef, cleared at controller's next turn.

Name: O'Donner Research Lab
Text: While in play, your O'Donner Research characters have +30 Max HP.
Flavor: The sign still says Wellness Institute.
Notes: Plague engine anchor; +30 Max HP to your O'Donner Research (sustains fodder under Plague).

Name: Patient Intake
Text: Search your deck for an O'Donner Research or Plagued character, reveal it, add it to hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: Every door opens inward.
Notes: Plague character tutor (affil-locked).

Name: Grant Proposal
Text: Search your deck for an O'Donner Research persistent event, reveal it, add it to hand, then shuffle.
Flavor: The grant is approved.
Notes: Lab tutor (event tutor - sim approximates with Field Promotion; not wired as a char tutor).

Name: Dispel
Text: Destroy a persistent event an opponent controls.
Flavor: The world-state is not as permanent as it pretends.
Notes: Categorical persistent-event removal - the answer the engine lacked (strips Plague / the Lab / a War). Names no card (Mathematical Opposition). Added 2026-06-25.
